steps.title: Identify the exponential decay structure
steps.content: The beam starts with $800$ photons at the surface. Each $7$ millimeters cuts the number in half, so the decay factor per $7$ mm is $0.5$. The number of 7-mm intervals in $x$ millimeters is $\frac{x}{7}$.

steps.title: Use a direct table calculation check
steps.content: The first few model points are $(0,800)$, $(7,400)$, and $(14,200)$. These confirm the starting value $800$ and the repeated multiplier $0.5$ every $7$ millimeters, so the model is $$I=800(0.5)^{x/7}$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice B halves the beam every $1$ mm, not every $7$ mm. The growth models use bases greater than $1$, so they increase instead of decay. D has initial value $800$, decay factor $0.5$, and exponent $x/7$, so it is correct.
